rulings.Case Title: CHUCK AMITAL, SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE FRED S. VS. NORMANDY APARTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT NORMANDY APARTMENTS, BAUM FAMILY PARTNERSHIP, BAUM-NORMANDY LLC,, MICHAEL BAUM, ROBERTA BAUM'S Amended Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER PECLUDING THE TAKING OF THE DEPOSITION OF WALTER RICCI AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF SEEKING SUCH DISCOVERY IF HIS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHSTANDS THE PENDING MOTION TO STRIKE AND DEMURRER. THE EARLIER ORDERS OF THIS COURT PRECLUDE THE DISCOVERY THAT PLAINTIFF NOW SEEKS AND UNTIL THERE IS A DETERMINATION AS TO THE APPROPRIATENESS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT THERE IS NO BASIS FOR IGNORING THESE PRIOR ORDERS. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON SEPT. 30, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: GF CONSTRUCTION CO. INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ANTHONY S. KWONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Permitting Destructive Testing To Occur Nothwithstanding The Expiration Of The Fact Discovery Cutoff, Or In The Alternative, To Reopen Fact Discovery For The Purpose Of Destructive Testing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT ANTHONY KWONG, MANDY KWONG'S Motion For An Order Permitting Destructive Testing To Occur Nothwithstanding The Expiration Of The Fact Discovery Cutoff, Or In The Alternative, To Reopen Fact Discovery For The Purpose Of Destructive Testing. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO PERMIT DESTRUCTIVE TESTING AT THEIR PREMISES IS GRANTED; THEIR MOTION TO RE-OPEN FACT DISCOVERY IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' EXPERT WAS IDENTIFED WITHIN THE TIME PERIOD SPECIFIED IN THE CODE AND NOTHING IN THE COURT'S ORDER CONTINUING THE TRIAL DATE PRECLUDED THE EXPERT'S CONTINUING INVESTIGATION AND PREPARATION OF HIS REPORT. PLAINTIFF'S REFUSAL TO AGREE TO LET THE TESTING PROCEED WAS UNJUSTIFIED. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,500.00 WILL BE IMPOSED AS AGAINST PLAINTIFF. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: WENJIE HUA VS. DAVID MING YOUNG MD et al (Defts: Regents' Defts Challenge Judge Goldsmith)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Extend Time For Filing And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF WENJIE HUA Motion To Extend Time For Filing And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs. The court denies the motion to extend. Under CRC 3.1700(b)(3), the court has the power to extend the time to file a motion to tax. That means that the plaintiff needed to seek an extension before the time to file a motion to tax lapsed. There is no authority that allows the court to revive the ability to file a motion to tax once the time period lapses. This conclusion is reinforced by CRC 3.1700(b)(4), which states that the clerk "must immediately" enter the costs on the judgment when the time period has passed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(AJR)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY LUDWIGSEN VS. MILLENNIUM PARTNERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 18, DEFENDANT MILLENNIUM PARTNERS, MILLENNIUM PARTNERS SPORTS CLUB MANAGEMENT LLC., ERIKA VARGAS DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Tentative Ruling: Demurrer sustained with leave to amend in part and denied in part. Defendant's demurrer to the first and eighth causes of action for wrongful discharge in violation of public policy is sustained with ten days leave to amend to add the specific OSHA violations which Plaintiffs allude to in the complaint. Defendant's demurrer to the tenth cause of action, which pertains to violation of Labor Code section 6310 is also sustained with ten days leave to amend to add the specific OSHA violations. Plaintiffs have not and are unable to plead facts which allow them to bring a claim under Labor Code section 1102.5(a-c). Thus, Defendant's demurrer to the second and ninth causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action for sexual harassment and a hostile work environment is sustained with ten days leave to amend to specify the pervasiveness and severity of the alleged harassment and to plead specific facts regarding the hostility that resulted from the harassment. Defendant's demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Defendant's demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Because Plaintiff Ludwigsen has been granted with leave to plead facts regarding the sexual harassment and discrimination, defendant's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination, harassment, and retaliation is also sustained with ten days leave to amend. Defendant's demurrer to the sixth and eleventh causes of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend in order to plead that proper notice was provided under Labor Code section 2699.3(a)(1). The demurrer to the seventh and sixteenth causes of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend in order to meet the class action requirements. Defendant's demurrer to the twelfth cause of action is sustained with tens days leave to amend in order to allow Plaintiff Ludwigsen to add specific facts showing extreme emotional suffering. Defendant's demurrer to the thirteenth, fourteenth, and fifteenth causes of action is denied. Plaintiff Ludwigsen's deposition statements cannot be considered in Defendant's demurrer. Defendant's demurrer to the seventeenth and eighteenth causes of action is denied. Plaintiff Ludwigsen filed her claims within the statute of limitations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BA BOTTOM LINE, LLC VS. SAFEDOCS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash The Production Of Documents Or Tangible Things At A Deposition And To Quash The Subpoena For The Personal Appearance Of Corporate Designated Witnesses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT SAFEDOCS INC., TERRY VAN BIBBER, SHIRLEY VAN BIBBER, RUSS DAVIS, KURT NEUMANN'S Motion To Quash The Production Of Documents Or Tangible Things At A Deposition And To Quash The Subpoena For The Personal Appearance Of Corporate Designated Witnesses. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: MOTION TO QUASH THE PRODUCTION OF RECORDS AND QUASH THE SUBPOENA OF CORPORATE WITNESS (CONTINUED FROM SEPT. 25, 2015 CALENDAR.) DEFENDANT SAFEDOCS' MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF RECORDS AND PERSONAL APPEARANCE OF CORPORATE WITNESSES OF THIRD PARTY, QUIMBIK, INC. IS GRANTED. IT APPEARS UNDISPUTED THAT DEFENDANT SAFEDOC'S CONTACT WITH QUIMBIK, INC FIRST TOOK PLACE MONTHS AFTER PLAINTIFF MADE ITS INVESTMENT IN DEFENDANT AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE, NOR ALLEGATION IN THE COMPLAINT THAT ANY REPRESENTATION WAS MADE CONCERNING QUIMBIK AT THE TIME OF PLAINTIFF'S INVESTMENT. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED, COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON SEPT. 30, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF BA BOTTOM LINE, LLC'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, And, For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $4,185 Against Defendants Safedocs, Inc., Terry Van Bibber, Russ Davis, Shirley Van Bibber, And Kurt Neumann And Their Attorney, Geoffrey D. rulings.Case Title: ERIC BOICE et al VS. HARCOURT GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILIT et al (Pltfs Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Eric Boice, Vinetta Boice And Yowie Stromberg'S Motion For Class Certification
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 8, Plaintiffs Eric Boice, Vinetta Boice And Yowie Stromberg'S Motion For Class Certification. The motion for class certification is denied. Individual issues predominate as to the actual damages of each class member and the reasons why each member vacated either one day after moving in or twenty eight days later. An individual action would be better than a class action given these proof issues. An individual has an incentive to bring his/her own claim given the ability to recover statutory and actual damages. Eric Boice and Yowie Stromberg are not proper class representatives because they do not appear to have any relationship with the landlord and were merely living at the premises. The proposed class is overbroad as it dates back to 2008. The statute of limitations is three years pursuant to CCP 338 and the continuing violation doctrine does not apply. That is a doctrine in employment law and cannot be used to revive stale claims in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(AJR)

rulings.Case Title: PHAT CHI TRAN VS. GEORGE W. BUSH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT BRIAN SCHLESINGER'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THERE APPEARS TO BE NO OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION, ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT WILL SIGN DEFENDANT BRIAN SCHELSINGER'S ORDER DISMISSING THIS ACTION WITH PREJUDICE. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: WORKERS COMPENSATION SOLUTIONS, L.L.C. VS. DANIEL ROMANSKI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 16, CROSS DEFENDANT WORKERS COMPENSATION SOLUTIONS, L.L.C. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is denied. Cross-Plaintiff correctly points out that California law permits pleading inconsistent theories of liability. As Cross-Plaintiff notes, "[o]ne of the typical occasions for alternative pleading is uncertainty about the nature of a contractual obligation that the evidence may establish: Express, implied in fact, or quasi-contractual." (4 Witkin, Cal. Procedure (5th Ed. 2008) Pleading, § 407.) Cross-P believes that it will be able to bring a successful breach of contract claim against Cross-Defendant for Cross-Defendant's breach of the settlement agreement. However, if facts emerge which indicate that the settlement agreement is not enforceable, Cross-Plaintiff is allowed to assert the underlying causes of actions as an alternative means of establishing liability. Cross-Defendant's reliance on Village Northridge is misplaced because Cross-Plaintiff is not alleging a claim of fraud in the inducement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SMULE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. MARK GODFREY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Smule, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2015, Line 6, Plaintiff Smule, Inc.'s Motion To Compel. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THE COURT FINDS THAT TO THE EXTENT THAT CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, SECTION 2019.210 IS APPLICABLE TO SOMEOF THE CLAIMS ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF, THE REQUIREMENTS OF THAT SECTION HAVE BEEN SATISFIED BY PLAINTIFF'S AUGUST 26, 2015 FILING. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: 1. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO REQUESTS NO. 1, 2 & 3 2. MOTION IS GRANTED AS REQUESTS NO. 4 THROUGH 36. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THESE REQUESTS ARE OVERRULED, EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT ASSERTS THAT ANY DOCUMENTS REQUESTED IS SUBJECT TO THE ATTORNEY CLIENT/WORK PRODUCT PRIVILEGE IT WILL PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH A PRIVILEGE LOG IDENTIFYING THE DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ENABLE PLAINTIF TO CHALLENGE THE ASSERT OF PRIVILEGE. PRIOR TO THE PRODUCTION OF ANY DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO THE TERMS OF THIS ORDER THE PARTIES WILL MEET AND CONFER FOR THE PURPOSE OF AGREEING UPON A FORM OF PROTECTIVE ORDER TO GOVERN THE HANDLING OF THE DOCUMENTS PRODUCED BY EITHER PARTY IN THIS LITIGATION. IF THEY ARE UNABLE TO REACH AGREEMENT WITHIN SEVEN (7) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, THE PARTIES WILL PROVIDE THE COURT WITH THEIR RESPECTIVE PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER AND THE COURT WILL FORTHWITH ENTER A PROTECTIVE ORDER TO GOVERN THE HANDLING OF THE DOCUMENTS PRODUCED. DEFENDANT WILL MAKE THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR BY PARAGRAPH 2 ABOVE NO LATER THAN THIRTY (30) FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR AGREEMENT AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 30, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
